description:
Goal
Replace compiler2 semantic-analysis local pending types with explicit value evidence before call resolution is rebuilt on ReturnEvidence. A local value can be known, explicitly dynamic, known-empty/no-return, or unavailable because a required fact is absent; that state must be represented by a semantic evidence enum, not by `Option<Ty>` fields or type sentinels.

Signal
- `SemanticValue { flow_ty: Option<Ty>, observed_ty: Option<Ty> }` is gone or reduced to a named evidence payload whose variants make impossible states unrepresentable.
- Local expression typing, value projection, branch merging, tuple/list/map construction, direct calls, closure calls, and delivered control-flow values pattern-match on evidence variants rather than checking missing `Option<Ty>`.
- The focused readiness test proves a missing earlier call result still allows publication of later activation demands when the later work does not need the missing type.
- A second test proves a type consumer waits or withholds its output when the value evidence is unavailable, instead of receiving `any`, `none`, or an empty `Option` projection.
- No published fact uses a local pending value as a semantic result. Pending/unavailable evidence remains job-local and is eliminated by waiting or by producing less output.

Strategy
- Build on the ReturnEvidence payload introduced by `.6.1`; do not invent a parallel readiness model.
- Data-model-up: make `Known` carry the exact flow/observed type pair, make explicit dynamic/no-return states named, and make unavailable evidence non-projectable by construction.
- Update `apply_step`, `analyze_tail`, `deliver_tail_value`, `merge_value_types`, and call emission coalescing so the compiler says exactly what evidence it has and what evidence is missing.
- Keep the scheduler boundary unchanged: facts are absent/current/settled; this ticket only makes semantic payloads honest inside the jobs that produce those facts.

Outro
No `Option<Ty>` as local readiness, no `SemanticValue::pending` that can be projected into a type by accident, no helper named like a compatibility shim, no missing value defaulting to `any`, and no bottom-as-unavailable path.
title: Compiler2 semantic evidence: make local value readiness explicit
